blocks.content: The idea came to him on a Sunday morning in May.

He was at the kitchen window — one cup, the yard going into its spring, the first green coming back — and he was thinking about her the way he thought about her all the time now. A background presence he had stopped trying to contain. It was everywhere. So was she.
He was thinking about the park.
The fountain. The bench. The wall beside the fountain where the afternoon light hit at a particular angle.

He had painted murals before. Not many — it wasn't his primary work — but he understood scale. He understood how something small in a sketchbook could become something that filled a wall. He understood how to make something that the world could see.
He stood at the window for a long time.
Then he set down his cup.
